source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/01_DHF/05_HnX-P1, HnX-PB/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/08_Cybersecurity/260303 사용목적 수정(CE 반영)/5. A-CTMR-HNX_Threat Modeling Report(Rev.5) .docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Threat modeling report for the Portable X-ray System models HnX-P1 and HnX-PB.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Provides cybersecurity lifecycle risk analysis, threat modeling, risk assessment, and mitigation implementation for the device.
keyPoints: The document aims to provide cybersecurity lifecycle risk analysis and threat modeling for Portable X-ray System(HnX-P1, HnX-PB).
The variant model HnX-PB has only changed the exterior design compared to the main model HnX-P1, and no additional cybersecurity risk management is performed due to the addition of the variant model.
STRIDE methodology is selected for identifying Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, and Elevation of Privilege.
Risk identification includes data-flow diagram risks, IEC 60601-4-5 cybersecurity requirement risks, risk assessment, and mitigation implementation.
After mitigation measures were taken for a total of 59 risks, residual risk was reduced to Low 3 and Very Low 56.

concepts.name: Cybersecurity lifecycle risk analysis
concepts.evidence: This document aims to provide cybersecurity lifecycle risk analysis and threat modeling for Portable X-ray System(HnX-P1, HnX-PB).

concepts.name: Threat modeling
concepts.evidence: The threat modeling document for the Medical Device HnX-P1 and HnX-PB ... aims to evaluate potential cybersecurity threats and pinpoint vulnerabilities within the device.

concepts.name: Data-Flow Diagram
concepts.evidence: A Data-Flow Diagram (DFD) in medical device cybersecurity is a graphical representation that maps out the flow of data within a medical system.

concepts.name: STRIDE methodology
concepts.evidence: The STRIDE methodology is selected for its comprehensiveness in identifying Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, and Elevation of Privilege.

concepts.name: IEC 60601-4-5 cybersecurity requirements
concepts.evidence: Risk identification according to cybersecurity requirements (IEC 60601-4-5)

concepts.name: Cybersecurity controls
concepts.evidence: Security control Risk ID Authentication ... Authorization ... Cryptography ... Code, Data, and Execution Integrity ... Confidentiality ... Event Detection and Logging ... Resiliency and Recovery ... Firmware and Software Updates

concepts.name: SBOM-based vulnerability scan
concepts.pageTarget: Software bill of materials vulnerability management
concepts.evidence: SBOM-based vulnerability scan (using CycloneDX format): Create SBOM in CycloneDX format and periodically review whether each component has CVE using automated tools such as Snyk/Syft

concepts.name: Bluetooth cybersecurity
concepts.evidence: It has Bluetooth functionality, but is designed to restrict connection of unauthorized devices.

concepts.name: Firmware integrity verification
concepts.evidence: The product provides an update function through the USB C-TYPE port, and the Likelihood for Risk is mitigated through integrity check during update.

evidence.claim: The report applies to Portable X-ray System models HnX-P1 and HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText:

Model name: HnX-P1, HnX-PB

evidence.claim: The document was prepared, reviewed, and approved on December 10, 2025.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared by Han, Jimin R&D team December 10, 2025 ... Reviewed by Hur, Namkyu Quality Management Representative December 10, 2025 ... Approved by Jeon, Jeu R&D manager December 10, 2025

evidence.claim: The document provides cybersecurity lifecycle risk analysis and threat modeling for HnX-P1 and HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: This document aims to provide cybersecurity lifecycle risk analysis and threat modeling for Portable X-ray System(HnX-P1, HnX-PB).

evidence.claim: HnX-PB is a variant model with exterior design changes only compared to HnX-P1.
evidence.evidenceText: The variant model HnX-PB has only changed the exterior design compared to the main model HnX-P1, and the interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, software, etc. are all the same.

evidence.claim: No additional cybersecurity risk management is performed due to the addition of HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, no additional cybersecurity risk management is performed due to the addition of the variant model (HnX-PB).

evidence.claim: Assets include data and functions, external ports, communications, firmware, and open source SBOM items.
evidence.evidenceText: Assets was identified for data and functions, external ports, communications, and firmware and open source of SBOM.

evidence.claim: User identification and authentication were not applied because the LCD is a non-touch display, with physical controls implemented through limited environment and users.
evidence.evidenceText: The LCD of the product is a non-touch display, so user identification and authentication were not applied, and physical control measures were implemented through a limited environment and limited users.

evidence.claim: The threat modeling uses STRIDE methodology.
evidence.evidenceText: Threat Methodology: The STRIDE methodology is selected for its comprehensiveness in identifying Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, and Elevation of Privilege

evidence.claim: The report includes IEC 60601-4-5 compliance testing as part of the threat modeling summary.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-4-5 Compliance Testing (RT): Ensures medical devices meet requirement for cybersecurity (35 initial risks)

evidence.claim: The report includes CWE vulnerability assessment.
evidence.evidenceText: CWE Vulnerability Assessment (VT): Detects and analyzes weaknesses to prevent cyber threats (56 initial risks)

evidence.claim: The report includes penetration testing.
evidence.evidenceText: Penetration Testing (PT): Simulates attacks to identify and fix security vulnerabilities in systems. (4 initial risks)

evidence.claim: Data-flow diagram risk identification lists risks CRM-01 through CRM-24.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk identification according to Data-Flow Diagram ... CRM-01 ... CRM-24

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-4-5 cybersecurity requirement risk identification lists risks CRM-25 through CRM-59.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk identification according to cybersecurity requirements (IEC 60601-4-5) ... CRM-25 ... CRM-59

evidence.claim: Mitigation controls are assigned to authentication, authorization, cryptography, integrity, confidentiality, event detection and logging, resiliency and recovery, and firmware/software updates.
evidence.evidenceText: Security control Risk ID Authentication ... Authorization ... Cryptography ... Code, Data, and Execution Integrity ... Confidentiality ... Event Detection and Logging ... Resiliency and Recovery ... Firmware and Software Updates

evidence.claim: Bluetooth communication uses SHA-256 packet integrity protection.
evidence.evidenceText: The product provides a Bluetooth communication function and provides a packet integrity protection function through data hashing (SHA-256) when transmitting/receiving Bluetooth packets.

evidence.claim: The device provides updates through a USB C-TYPE port with integrity checks during update.
evidence.evidenceText: The product provides an update function through the USB C-TYPE port, and the Likelihood for Risk is mitigated through integrity check during update.

evidence.claim: The device is not connected to an Ethernet network and Bluetooth connection is limited.
evidence.evidenceText: The device is not connected to an Ethernet network, and connection is limited during Bluetooth communication.

evidence.claim: After mitigation, residual risk was reduced to Low 3 and Very Low 56.
evidence.evidenceText: After mitigation measures were taken for a total of 59 risks (Low: 7 /Medium: 31 / High: 17 / Critical: 4), the residual risk was reduced to a total of Low 3 & Very Low: 56.

reviewFlags.reason: No explicit regulatory authority or jurisdiction is identified in the source text; source metadata only states authoritySignal: general.
The threat modeling summary states 'In total 16 risks' but the risk identification and final summary list 59 risks, creating conflicting evidence.
Several OCR or transcription issues appear in the source text, including 'Attemps', 'Tempering', and spacing inconsistencies in open source component names.
